question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked zero didactic questions. The roles were effectively inverted — the User presented two solutions and asked the Pair to choose, and the Pair simply picked A and wrote code. No teachable moments were surfaced by the Pair to the User.

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed by the Pair. The only 'choice' (sorted-key vs count-key) was authored by the User, and the Pair just selected one with a one-line justification rather than probing trade-offs, asking about complexity, or surfacing the 26-char alphabet assumption in Option B.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no progression of questions to scaffold. The Pair jumped straight to a complete solution with tests in a single turn; the user never had to reason about hashing keys, tuple-vs-list hashability, or edge cases.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the prose is clean, but the conversational dynamic is broken — the Pair behaves as the student answering the User's prompts rather than guiding them. The exchange terminates in two turns with no real back-and-forth.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
